title: Water on the Road
description: A trade caravan has been camped at Sandrift's edge for nine days. A man in gold armor sits on the road east and will not let anyone pass without 'proof of legitimate business.' The road did not have a checkpoint before the cave.

offer.speaker: Caravan Master Kes
offer.text: The road's been closed nine days. Not by weather. By a man in gold armor who won't move until we present something he calls 'proof of legitimate business.' Which is not, historically, how roads work.

offer.speaker: Caravan Master Kes
offer.text: He carries paperwork. It's new paperwork. The seals on it are new. It cites laws that we have looked up and cannot find. Nobody around here has that kind of ink.

accept.speaker: Caravan Master Kes
accept.text: Good. Take my papers — they're worthless, he's rejected them four times. But you can offer him a story instead. He seems to want a story more than a stamp.

accept.speaker: Caravan Master Kes
accept.text: You came from the cave? Then you have a story that stamps itself. Try that one.

decline.speaker: Caravan Master Kes
decline.text: Fine. The camels have opinions about the delay. I have run out of opinions.

in_progress.speaker: Caravan Master Kes
in_progress.text: Nine days become ten. The camels are past patient. I am past useful advice.

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Caravan Master Kes
ready_to_turn_in.text: The road is quiet. The road has not been quiet in a very long time. That's the sound of you having done something.

complete.speaker: Caravan Master Kes
complete.text: You did — what did you do — never mind. The road is open. Take this. It's not much. It's what a caravan pays when a caravan can pay again.

complete.speaker: Caravan Master Kes
complete.text: One thing. The paperwork he was carrying — it wasn't drafted for him. Someone drafted it, then handed it to a soldier and told him to be a lawyer. That someone is who I would like a word with. But not today.

objectives.description: Hear Caravan Master Kes out about the blocked road.
Meet the man on the road east of Sandrift. Present a story he can accept.
Return to Kes at the Sandrift bazaar.

cross_world_connection: The Warden's paperwork uses the same phrasing that later appears on the council-chamber desks in Castle Harmonia ('authority derives from —' + blank). Someone at the capital drafted both, and handed one to a soldier and told him to be a lawyer.
_wiring_notes: Step 2 uses w1_warden_defeated as a shared flag with the Warden encounter (Lane B places the Masterite; Lane C consumes their defeat). If the Warden encounter isn't placed yet, this step is unreachable — quest sits at active-step-2, gracefully. Kes spawn on Sandrift entry; no prereq_flag needed at load, but a prereq of cutscene_flag_rat_king_defeated is recommended so the quest appears AFTER the cave is beaten (empty spawn_flag = always visible, which is fine if Sandrift entry itself is gated). Design doc: docs/design/w1-progression-expansion.md, Lane C row 1.
